section_title: VXLAN VNI Mismatch — Diagnosis and Verification
subsection_title: VLAN-VNI mapping consistency across fabric devices
text: A VNI operational state down condition on Arista EOS typically indicates one of three root causes: the VLAN-VNI mapping is missing or incorrect in the VXLAN interface configuration, the NVE (Network Virtualization Edge) interface is not bound to the correct source interface, or the VNI is provisioned on some but not all fabric devices causing asymmetric forwarding. Verification steps: (1) confirm 'show vxlan vni' output matches the intended VLAN-VNI mapping, (2) verify the NVE source interface is up and has a reachable IP, (3) check that the VNI is consistently provisioned across all leaf devices in the same flooding domain. The EVPN control plane will not advertise type-2 or type-3 routes for a VNI that is operationally down.

subsection_title: NVE interface state and VNI operational verification
text: On Cisco NX-OS, VNI operational state is exposed via 'show nve vni' and 'show nve peers'. A VNI in 'down' or 'CP_DOWN' state indicates the control plane has not fully provisioned the VNI. Common causes include: the interface nve1 not being configured with the member vni statement, the VLAN not being extended to the NVE interface, or the BGP EVPN address family not advertising routes for the affected VNI. The 'show bgp l2vpn evpn' command confirms whether type-2 and type-3 routes are present. For VNI mismatches between leaf devices, compare the VNI-to-VLAN mapping in the nve interface configuration across all affected devices. Configuration changes to the nve interface require care as they affect all VNIs in the same flooding domain.

subsection_title: VNI provisioning and EVPN instance binding verification
text: On Juniper QFX platforms, VNI operational state is tied to the EVPN instance configuration. A VNI that is down or not present in 'show evpn instance' output indicates the vni-options stanza is missing or the EVPN instance is not bound to the correct routing instance. Junos requires explicit VNI-to-VLAN mapping in the evpn stanza under the bridge-domain. Asymmetric VNI provisioning — where leaf-01 has VNI 10100 mapped to VLAN 100 but leaf-02 maps the same VLAN to VNI 10101 — silently breaks layer-2 forwarding without generating a BGP error. Diagnosis requires comparing the evpn instance vni-list across all leaf devices in the same flooding domain.
